DISCOVERY 302, MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFFS FORM INTERROGATORIES - GENERAL, SET ONE; REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT OAKVILLE SHALL PROVIDE FURTHER COMPLETE AND RESPONSIVE ANSWERS TO THE INTERROGATORIES IN QUESTION VERIFIED UNDER OATH WITHOUT OBJECTION EXCEPT AS TO PRIVILEGE AND WORK PRODUCT AS TO WHICH, IF CLAIMED, DEFENDANT OAKVILLE SHALL PROVIDE A DETAILED PRIVILEGE LOG, ALL WITHIN 20 DAYS OF THE DATE OF ORDER. DEFENDANT OAKVILLE AND ITS ATTORNEYS OF RECORD, JOINTLY AND SEVERALLY, SHALL, WITHIN 20 DAYS OF THE DATE OF ORDER, PAY $1250 TO PLAINTIFF, REPRESENTING SANCTIONS FOR MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS INCLUDING VIOLATION OF CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 2023.010 (A), (D), (E), (F) AND (I). PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE FORM OF ORDER THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE PRO TEM: JAMES FLEMING; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; NOT REPORTED; DEPT. 302.

ORDER GRANTING MOTION BY PLAINTIFF NG SOLUTIONS, LLC TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES FROM DEFENDANT AARON WHEELER TO FORM INTERROGATORIES - GENERAL, SET ONE

DISCOVERY 302,PLAINTIFF NG SOLUTIONS, LLC'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES FROM DEFENDANT AARON WHEELER TO FORM INTERROGATORIES GENERAL, SET ONE REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT WHEELER SHALL PROVIDE AMENDED, VERIFIED RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES 2.6 AND 17.1 (AS TO EACH RESPONSE TO RFA THAT WAS NOT AN UNQUALIFIED ADMISSION) WITHIN ONE WEEK OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,560 ARE DUE AND PAYABLE TO PLAINTIFF AND PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL IF VERIFIED FULL AND COMPLETE RESPONSES ARE NOT RECEIVED WITHIN 10 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM: PETER CATALANOTTI: J. RIOS MERIDA; NOT REPORTED. (DISC302)

OPPOSITION TO MOTION FOR AWARD OF ATTORNEYS FEES (TRANSACTION ID # 100056409) FILED BY DEFENDANT OAKVILLE CAPITAL, INC. WHEELER, AARON T.
MINI MINUTES FOR DEC-17-2018 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF NG SOLUTIONS, LLC'S MOTION FOR AN AWARD OF ATTORNEY'S FEES IS GRANTED. NG IS AWARDED $52,840 IN FEES AGAINST DEFENDANTS OAKVILLE CAPITAL, INC. AND AARON WHEELER. NG HAS SHOWN IT IS ENTITLED TO AN AWARD OF FEES AS THE PREVAILING PARTY IN THIS CASE PER THE FEES PROVISION IN THE PARTIES' AGREEMENT. THE HOURLY RATES AND ASSERTED TIME SPENT OF NG'S COUNSEL IS REASONABLE AND COMPORTS WITH THE ACTIVITIES UNDERTAKEN BY NG'S COUNSEL. THE TIME SPENT BY NG'S COUNSEL IS ADEQUATELY SUPPORTED BY THE COMPOSITE STATEMENT OF ACCOUNT ATTACHED AS EXHIBIT B TO MR. STROM'S DECLARATION. WHILE DEFENDANTS CONTEND THAT MOST OR SOME OF THE SERVICES PERFORMED BY NG'S COUNSEL WAS UNNECESSARY, DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT IDENTIFIED ANY SPECIFIC LITIGATION ACTIVITY THAT WAS EITHER UNNECESSARY, UNREASONABLE OR TOOK LONGER THAN IT SHOULD HAVE TAKEN. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO CSR#8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM (302/HEK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Award Of Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Dec-17-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Mark N. Strom, Esq., Versus Law Group, Telephone 310-453-5053, appearing for Petitioner; (CourtCall)
No other appearances;

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff NG Solutions, LLC's motion for an award of attorney's fees is granted. NG is awarded $52,840 in fees against defendants Oakville Capital, Inc. and Aaron Wheeler. NG has shown it is entitled to an award of fees as the prevailing party in this case per the fees provision in the parties' agreement. The hourly rates and asserted time spent of NG's counsel is reasonable and comports with the activities undertaken by NG's counsel. The time spent by NG's counsel is adequately supported by the composite statement of account attached as exhibit B to Mr. Strom's declaration. While defendants contend that most or some of the services performed by NG's counsel was unnecessary, defendants have not identified any specific litigation activity that was either unnecessary, unreasonable or took longer than it should have taken. Prevailing party to prepare order and to comply with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/HEK)

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF NG SOLUTIONS, LLC's Motion To Compel Further Responses From Defendant Aaron Wheeler To Form Interrogatories General, Set One Request For Sanctions is GRANTED as follows: Defendant Wheeler shall provide amended, verified responses to Form Interrogatories 2.6 and 17.1 (as to each response to RFA that was not an unqualified admission) within one week of notice of this order. Sanctions in the amount of $1,560 are due and payable to plaintiff and plaintiff's counsel if verified full and complete responses are not received within 10 days of notice of this order. Off Calendar Sep-12-2017 Continued To Nov-01-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Form Interrogatories - General, Set One; Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-07-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Mark N. Strom, of Verus Law Group, Tel. 310.453.5053, for plaintiffs, appearing via CourtCall;
No other appearances.

Ruling: The matter was discussed. PLAINTIFF NG SOLUTIONS, LLC, Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Form Interrogatories - General, Set One; Request For Sanctions is granted. Defendant Oakville shall provide further complete and responsive answers to the interrogatories in question verified under oath without objection except as to privilege and work product as to which, if claimed, defendant Oakville shall provide a detailed privilege log, all within 20 days of the date of order. Defendant Oakville and its attorneys of record, jointly and severally, shall, within 20 days of the date of order, pay $1250 to plaintiff, representing sanctions for misuse of the discovery process including violation of Code of Civil Procedure Section 2023.010 (a), (d), (e), (f) and (i). Prevailing party shall prepare form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge Pro Tem: James Fleming; Clerk: Felicia Green; Not reported; Dept. 302.
